<doctag><picture><loc_35><loc_34><loc_63><loc_45></picture>
<section_header_level_1><loc_36><loc_61><loc_210><loc_70>1 Functional description</section_header_level_1>
<text><loc_82><loc_80><loc_460><loc_119>The STUSB4500 is a USB Type-C™ and power delivery controller IC for sink applications. It is able to negotiate a power delivery contract with a source without MCU support (auto-run mode). It relies on proprietary algorithms and configurable PDO (power data objects) thanks to an integrated non-volatile memory. It supports dead battery mode to allow a system to be powered from an external source directly. Combined with its capability to negotiate directly a power contract, the STUSB4500 is the ideal controller device for autonomous systems requiring high power charging profile to be fully operational.</text>
<section_header_level_1><loc_83><loc_121><loc_199><loc_127>The STUSB4500 major role is to:</section_header_level_1>
<unordered_list><list_item><loc_83><loc_129><loc_347><loc_135>1. Detect the connection between two USB Type-C ports (attach detection)</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_137><loc_248><loc_143>2. Establish a valid source-to-sink connection</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_145><loc_324><loc_151>3. Determine the attached device mode: source or debug accessory</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_153><loc_414><loc_159>4. Resolve cable orientation and twist connections to establish USB data routing (MUX control)</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_161><loc_371><loc_167>5. Negotiate a USB power delivery (PD) contract with a PD capable source device</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_169><loc_362><loc_175>6. Configure the incoming V$_{BUS}$ power path and the charging paths accordingly</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_178><loc_342><loc_184>7. Monitor the V$_{BUS}$ power path and manage the V$_{BUS}$ voltage transitions</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_187><loc_224><loc_192>8. Handle the high voltage protections</list_item>
</unordered_list>
<section_header_level_1><loc_83><loc_194><loc_194><loc_200>The STUSB4500 also provides:</section_header_level_1>
<unordered_list><list_item><loc_83><loc_202><loc_170><loc_208>· Dead battery mode</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_210><loc_284><loc_216>· PDO (power data object) customization through NVM</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_218><loc_258><loc_224>· Internal and/or external V$_{BUS}$ discharge paths</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_226><loc_236><loc_232>· Dual high power charging path support</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_234><loc_218><loc_240>· Debug accessory mode detection</list_item>
<list_item><loc_83><loc_242><loc_395><loc_248>· Customization of the device configuration through NVM to support specific applications</list_item>
</unordered_list>
<section_header_level_1><loc_36><loc_258><loc_157><loc_265>1.1 Block overview</section_header_level_1>
<picture><loc_131><loc_296><loc_418><loc_431><caption><loc_211><loc_277><loc_337><loc_283>Figure 1. Functional block diagram</caption></picture>
